title: Go 1.26 and AWS SDK for Go v2 Client Contract · Go Semantics Case: Runtime Sdk Version
summary: A service copies an SDK v1 tutorial, floats module versions, shares request options incorrectly, and treats generated Go types as runtime validation. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the runtime sdk version increment in pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 client contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.
Validate globally or zonally scoped naming, ownership, location, public-block, encryption, versioning, tags, and rollback before bucket creation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Runtime Sdk Version · Theory Runtime Sdk Version
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3185 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce pinned Go and AWS SDK v2 versions, client lifetime, runtime validation, and typed errors; cap bytes, time, attempts, concurrency, and spend before starting work; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Go 1.26.5, aws-sdk-go-v2 1.42.1, S3 1.105.0, CloudFront sign 1.11.7, and tested operating targets as one compatibility contract.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Runtime Sdk Version contract for the supplied data.
